target.title: fix(memory): honor qmd search timeout for memory_search
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`memory_search` had a fixed 15s outer deadline, but QMD-backed searches can legitimately take longer when `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` is raised. The old outer guard could time out the tool before QMD finished a configured search.

Fixes #91947.

## Why This Change Was Made

QMD search has a longer lifecycle than a single command timeout: direct multi-collection probes, search/query/vsearch attempts, unsupported-mode fallback, missing-collection repair, pending update wait, optional dirty sync, and timer grace can all be part of one user-visible search.

This PR keeps the timeout decision on the memory manager instead of mapping one QMD command timeout onto the whole tool. It also intentionally does not change QMD subprocess cancellation or `runCliCommand`; that remains separate from #93394.

## User Impact

QMD-backed `memory_search` can now complete when its configured QMD whole-search budget is longer than 15s.

Non-QMD behavior stays bounded by the default guard: runtime loading, manager setup, wiki supplements, builtin searches, and builtin forced-sync retry share the existing 15s deadline. If QMD fails and the wrapper falls back to the builtin index during the same call, that fallback search has its own 15s cap instead of inheriting the longer QMD budget.

No storage migration or data model change is required.

## Evidence

AI-assisted: yes. Generated with Codex/ZeroSignal and checked with local tests, live QMD behavior proof, and Codex review findings.

Real QMD-backed behavior proof on this PR head, run June 20, 2026 EDT:

- Harness used the repo-local `./node_modules/.bin/tsx --conditions=development` entrypoint.
- Created a temporary workspace with `MEMORY.md` and `memory/qmd-proof.md`.
- Configured `memory.backend = "qmd"`, `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs = 60000`, `searchMode = "search"`, and an explicit allow scope for the standalone harness.
- Wrapped the live `npx -y @tobilu/qmd` CLI with a script that sleeps 16s only for `search|query|vsearch` commands.

```json
{
  "configuredTimeoutMs": 60000,
  "oldOuterDeadlineMs": 15000,
  "elapsedMs": 24557,
  "disabled": false,
  "debug": {
    "backend": "qmd",
    "configuredMode": "search",
    "effectiveMode": "search",
    "searchMs": 17502,
    "hits": 1
  },
  "resultCount": 1,
  "results": [
    {
      "path": "memory/qmd-proof.md",
      "source": "memory",
      "corpus": "memory"
    }
  ]
}
```

Review findings addressed:

- ClawSweeper fallback deadline finding: QMD-to-builtin fallback now has a bounded 15s fallback search deadline, covered by `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts`.
- ClawSweeper cancellation overlap finding: the `qmd-process.ts` / `runCliCommand` cancellation changes were removed from this PR.
- Codex review P1/P2 findings: fallback search is separately bounded, and builtin forced-sync retry now spends the same default 15s deadline instead of getting fresh 15s windows.

Local validation:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts` -> 20 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts` -> 36 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts` -> 130 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts` -> 20 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts` -> 16 passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` -> passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json ...` -> passed
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed

Codex review status:

- `npx -y @openai/codex@latest review --base upstream/main -c model='"gpt-5.5"'` completed once and produced the fallback/shared-deadline findings addressed here.
- A clean re-run after the fixes was attempted at 11:38:59 EDT but was blocked by Codex usage limits until 3:43 PM EDT, so this PR does not claim a clean Codex re-review yet.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
